label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out which of the four options is true based on the given passage about computers and the Kurzweil Reading Machine. First, let's look at each option one by one. Option A: "Computers can solve all the problems people can't do." The passage starts by saying computers can solve difficult problems and control other machines. It also mentions scientists use large computers to solve many problems at once, and people use calculators for simple math. However, the word "all" here is a big clue. The passage doesn't say computers can solve every problem people can't do. It just lists some uses. So A is probably false because it's too absolute. Computers can't do everything, just many tasks. Option B: "Large computers can only solve one problem at a time." The passage says scientists use large computers to solve many problems at the same time. So if it says they can do many at once, then option B is the opposite. So B is definitely false. Option C: "When using the Kurzweil machine, a blind man needn't do anything." The passage explains the process where a blind person has to open the book, put it on the scanner, turn the page, and put it back. So they do need to do some actions. Therefore, C is incorrect because the user does have to perform steps like turning pages. Option D: "The Kurzweil machine can help blind people and short-sighted children."
